paper_title: Web-Based Volunteer Distributed Computing for Handling Time-Critical Urgent Workloads
paper_claims: Browser-based volunteer distributed computing using WebWorkers and a centralized cross-origin task manager can viably execute embarrassingly parallel urgent workloads without client-side software installation across heterogeneous platforms.

In web-based volunteer computing, a small minority of long dwell time sessions complete most tasks while many short sessions complete none, making performance dependent on value sessions and imposing overhead from non-value sessions.

Checkpointing partial results, batching tasks, and asynchronously prefetching new tasks increase the amount of useful work completed during volatile browsing sessions at the cost of higher server-side load.
Tuning task granularity is essential because smaller tasks increase the likelihood of value sessions but also amplify task management and communication overheads.

For data-bound workloads, XMLHttpRequest communication can outperform WebSockets, whereas compute-bound kernels are relatively insensitive to protocol choice and their browser-executed performance relative to native code depends on algorithm characteristics and browser engine optimizations.

paper_title: A topological analysis of cointegrated data: a Z24 Bridge case study
paper_claims: Time-delay embeddings of Z24 Bridge natural frequency time series exhibit a toroidal topology driven by cyclic temperature effects, whereas embeddings of cointegrated residuals exhibit an open-ball topology characteristic of approximately Gaussian white-noise processes.

The nonlinear dependence of the second natural frequency on temperature leads linear cointegration to leave residual topological structure, while Gaussian process-based nonlinear cointegration trained on data that include the nonlinear regime more effectively removes these effects.

Persistent homology comparisons using Wasserstein distances on Vietoris-Rips complexes of embedded series provide a quantitative topology-based metric for assessing the effectiveness of cointegration in detrending environmental and operational variability.

paper_title: Predicting Companies' ESG Ratings from News Articles Using Multivariate Timeseries Analysis
paper_claims: Annual corporate ESG ratings can be inferred from news by modeling monthly multivariate timeseries that integrate weakly supervised ESG-relevance detection, sentiment ratios for relevant and irrelevant articles, and semantic topic cluster frequencies.

Formulating ESG rating prediction as a regression task over these timeseries with deep convolutional architectures yields superior predictive performance compared to classification and to index-aggregation baselines based on document-level transformer outputs.

Weak supervision using sentence-embedding similarity to ESG definitions effectively bootstraps a DistilBERT-based classifier to generate scalable ESG-relevance signals without manual keyword selection.

A large-scale news corpus aligned to thousands of US companies and annual third‑party ESG scores over multiple years is compiled and publicly released to support training and evaluation of ESG prediction models.

paper_title: Semi-Supervised Learning of Monocular Depth Estimation via Consistency Regularization with K-way Disjoint Masking
paper_claims: A semi-supervised monocular depth estimation framework enforces uncertainty-weighted consistency between predictions from weakly and strongly augmented views generated via K-way disjoint masking, enabling effective learning from unlabeled images without stereo or video sequences.

K-way disjoint masking forms strong augmentations by independently encoding multiple disjoint token subsets and decoding their concatenation, which preserves global context, retains small objects, and mitigates scale ambiguity in depth prediction.

An auxiliary uncertainty estimation module weights pixelwise consistency to filter noisy pseudo-labels, while a predictor-headed feature consistency constraint stabilizes training and prevents collapse.
The approach remains robust under extremely sparse depth annotations and can be directly applied to unsupervised domain adaptation from synthetic to real data without additional generative models.

paper_title: Progressive Backdoor Erasing via connecting Backdoor and Adversarial Attacks
paper_claims: In a linear classifier with an additive patch trigger, the optimal untargeted adversarial perturbation has a substantial projection onto the trigger direction, theoretically explaining the similarity between adversarial and triggered inputs.

Fine-tuning an infected model on untargeted adversarial examples associates trigger-like patterns with multiple labels and thereby disrupts the trigger–target correlation that drives backdoor behavior while preserving performance on clean data.

Backdoors can be erased without a clean extra dataset by progressively alternating adversarial fine-tuning with clean-sample identification based on logit-similarity between infected and purified models to iteratively refine both the model and the auxiliary dataset.

paper_title: Difformer: Empowering Diffusion Model on Embedding Space for Text Generation
paper_claims: In embedding diffusion models for text, training with the standard denoising objective causes collapse of the learnable embedding space, producing anisotropic and poorly distinguishable token representations and destabilizing optimization.

An anchor loss that maximizes token likelihood given the denoiser’s predicted clean embeddings enforces well-distributed and distinguishable embeddings while stabilizing training by directly coupling denoiser outputs to discrete targets.

In high-dimensional embedding spaces, conventional noise schedules provide insufficient corruption that drives the denoising model to a condition-agnostic nearest-neighbor predictor, with the degenerate solution becoming globally optimal as noise vanishes and dimension grows.

A noise rescaling framework guided by a degeneration score that quantifies the accuracy of the degenerate predictor increases schedule variance via a multiplicative factor to prevent degeneration, independently of the base noise schedule.

A Transformer-based embedding diffusion model equipped with explicit length prediction and two-dimensional parallel decoding over candidate lengths and initial noises enables fast inference via reverse-step subsampling while maintaining high generation quality and diversity.

paper_title: How to measure uncertainty in uncertainty sampling for active learning
paper_claims: Separating predictive uncertainty into reducible epistemic and irreducible aleatoric components enables uncertainty sampling rules that target epistemic uncertainty as the more informative criterion.

Feature-wise evidence-based uncertainty measures disregard class priors and model aggregation, do not capture model uncertainty, and can therefore behave inconsistently with predictive entropy under class imbalance and linear models.
Uncertainty sampling with Naïve Bayes induces sampling bias that distorts estimated class priors toward frequently queried uncertain instances and can yield suboptimal decisions.
Credal uncertainty derived from probability intervals correlates strongly with epistemic uncertainty and poorly with aleatoric uncertainty, effectively acting as a measure of epistemic uncertainty.

Weighting candidate models by relative likelihood provides normalized degrees of class support that decompose total uncertainty into separate epistemic and aleatoric parts and afford graded plausibility advantages over hard credal sets.

paper_title: ReliefE: feature ranking in high-dimensional spaces via manifold embeddings
paper_claims: ReliefE performs feature ranking by computing neighbor relations in low-dimensional manifold embeddings of the instance space and, for multi-label tasks, the label space, enabling scalable operation on high-dimensional data via sparse-matrix computation.

The intrinsic dimensionality of the embedding space is estimated from the empirical distribution of ratios between distances to the two nearest nonidentical neighbors, eliminating manual selection of embedding dimension.
The number of neighbors used in the update step is selected adaptively per instance by identifying the largest gap in the sorted distance distribution, obviating a fixed k hyperparameter.
Updating feature weights against the average representation of the selected neighbors improves robustness to outliers compared to averaging per-neighbor updates.
Probabilistic matrix sparsification and representative subspace training reduce memory and time costs of embedding while maintaining the quality of the resulting feature rankings.

paper_claims: RoleSim* defines role-based similarity as a convex combination of average similarities over maximum bipartite matchings of in-neighbors and over the remaining in-neighbor pairs, thereby preserving automorphic equivalence while incorporating non-matching neighbor information.

paper_claims: A permutation-invariant neural network that ingests the contents of a training subset can predict the parameters of the model produced by a specified learning algorithm on that subset, generalizing to subsets with unseen points and enabling downstream tasks such as data valuation, data selection, memorization assessment, and prediction calibration.

paper_title: A MECS redistribution algorithm for SDN-enable MEC using response time and transmission overhead
paper_claims: In SDN-enabled MEC with multiple controllers, modeling controller response time as M/G/1 sojourn plus hop-based propagation and transmission overhead as hop-weighted request load, and solving the resulting bi-objective MECS-to-controller assignment via a decomposition-based evolutionary algorithm with coordinate-transformation selection, jointly reduces control-plane response time and data-plane PACKET_IN transmission overhead while balancing controller load under capacity and limited-migration constraints.

Capping the maximum number of concurrent MECS redistributions per step reduces search space and prevents transient controller resource spikes, enabling convergence to high-quality assignments within effective time.

Increasing the number of controllers lowers average response delay and transmission overhead only up to a topology- and load-dependent saturation point, after which additional controllers provide no benefit and may degrade performance in heterogeneous (scale-free) networks if topology is not considered.

paper_title: Hierarchical sparse Cholesky decomposition with applications to high-dimensional spatio-temporal filtering
paper_claims: A hierarchical Vecchia approximation defined by conditioning each hierarchical group on its nested ancestors yields a Gaussian-process model whose covariance and precision matrices have Cholesky factors that are both sparse, share the same sparsity pattern, and satisfy that the precision factor equals the inverse transpose of the covariance factor.

Under independent Gaussian observation noise, the posterior covariance and precision Cholesky factors induced by the hierarchical Vecchia prior retain exactly the same sparsity patterns as the prior factors.

The covariance Cholesky factor implied by the hierarchical Vecchia approximation equals the incomplete Cholesky factorization of the exact covariance restricted to the specified sparsity pattern, enabling inference that scales as O(n N^2) time and O(n N) memory when conditioning sets have size at most N.

Combining the hierarchical Vecchia structure with a Laplace approximation for exponential-family likelihoods yields a Newton–Raphson-based algorithm that exploits the same sparsity and converges under concavity to a Gaussian approximation at the posterior mode.

Embedding the hierarchical Vecchia structure into linear and linearized spatiotemporal state-space models produces Kalman–Vecchia–Laplace filters that preserve sparsity through forecast and update steps and scale approximately linearly with state dimension given sparse temporal evolution.

Matching vertices by comparing 1‑Wasserstein distances between degree profile distributions and connecting each vertex to its top‑d candidates accommodates partial overlap without enforcing a bijection and produces candidate match sets.

Seeding with high‑degree vertices and refining via iterative linear assignment that maximizes common neighbors converts candidate sets into one‑to‑one alignments and provides per‑vertex convergence diagnostics.
Combining spectral community detection based on ratios of eigenvectors with within‑community degree‑profile matching and post‑processing enables graph matching under stochastic block models.

Degree‑profile‑based matching operates in polynomial time with distance computation O(n^3ρ + n^2) and subsequent steps O(n^2) and remains effective in sparse graphs where spectral methods typically fail.

paper_title: Math-word embedding in math search and semantic extraction
paper_claims: Math-specific word embeddings trained on mathematical corpora capture semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions and can support tasks such as term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, and query expansion.

Representing mathematical expressions as streams of tokens enables learning of similarities among mathematical objects but is inadequate for reliably linking identifiers to textual definiens due to ambiguity and context dependence.

The lack of precise, canonical semantic specifications for identifiers and functions in MathML/OpenMath and the absence of standardized datasets and benchmarks are major obstacles to effective machine learning for mathematical information retrieval and semantic extraction.

Embedding entire equations as single tokens facilitates comparison of equations, whereas recovering the semantics of individual identifiers requires structural grouping and canonicalization beyond current embedding techniques.
Word2vec-style embeddings trained on math texts can exhibit non-symmetric similarity behavior, limiting reliability for automated end-to-end retrieval scenarios.

paper_title: PenQuest: a gamified attacker/defender meta model for cyber security assessment and education
paper_claims: A gamified attacker/defender meta model integrates service, information, and event layers with standardized knowledge bases (STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE) and NIST SP 800-53 controls to map concrete attack patterns and observable events to corresponding countermeasures for cyber risk assessment and education.

The rule system represents adversarial campaigns as a dynamic, asymmetric, non-cooperative, imperfect-information game with Bayesian elements, in which time-enabled actions affect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability status of interdependent services.

Parametric action definitions that include actor attributes, equipment effects, requirements, and success and detection chances, linked to attack patterns and event sequences, enable semantic correlation of monitoring data with attacker behavior and systematic selection of mitigating controls.

paper_title: Deep Insights into Convolutional Networks for Video Recognition
paper_claims: Cross-stream fusion in two-stream convolutional architectures induces units that integrate appearance and motion into coherent spatiotemporal features rather than independent cues.
The learned representation comprises both highly class-specific local detectors and generic units shared across classes, yielding distributed codes for action recognition.

With increasing network depth, feature selectivity becomes more abstract and increasingly invariant to nuisance factors such as motion speed, with motion units tuned to direction while tolerating a range of speeds and accelerations.

Early motion-stream layers implement spatiotemporal derivative-like filtering with temporally low-pass characteristics, whereas later layers capture higher-frequency temporal dynamics and exhibit stronger dependence on spatiotemporal regularization.

During supervised training, early-layer spatial filter structure remains stable while temporal selectivity progressively emerges, whereas higher-layer spatiotemporal filters undergo substantial structural changes.

paper_title: A hybrid model for EEG-based gender recognition
paper_claims: Entropy-based features of resting-state EEG exhibit systematic gender differences across multiple scalp sites, providing discriminative information for gender recognition.

A hybrid classifier that encodes Random Forest leaf indices as sparse features for Logistic Regression yields superior EEG-based gender recognition compared with either Random Forest or Logistic Regression alone.
Fuzzy entropy features from EEG provide the strongest gender-discriminative power among the tested entropy measures and can match or exceed the performance of combined entropy feature sets.

Classification performance increases with the number of EEG electrodes, and the hybrid model shows greater robustness to variations in training data size and male–female class ratio than individual classifiers.

paper_title: Refractive Two-View Reconstruction for Underwater 3D Vision
paper_claims: Refraction at a flat interface renders the single-viewpoint approximation invalid and induces depth-dependent geometric bias in underwater imaging when using pinhole-plus-distortion models.

In the case of a pinhole camera observing through a known thin, flat refractive interface, the two-view geometry admits a refractive fundamental matrix that depends on the interface’s position and orientation and the relative camera motion.

Using the refractive fundamental constraint together with refractive reprojection constraints in a two-step estimation improves outlier rejection and yields more accurate and robust underwater relative pose and quasi-dense 3D reconstruction than standard two-view Structure-from-Motion.

paper_title: A new design for cubic spatially processed wideband arrays
paper_claims: A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element can steer a fully spatial beam throughout full azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth.

Real-valued element weights are determined by representing the array response as a sum of L symmetric steering terms centered at selected focal points in a bounded three-dimensional frequency space and solving a reduced L-by-L linear system, where even L and symmetric point placement ensure real weights and unit gain in the desired direction.

The design requires solving a linear system of dimension L that can be chosen much smaller than the total number of array elements, thereby substantially reducing computational complexity relative to direct weight computation.

With inter-element spacings not exceeding half the minimum wavelength, the transformed frequency variables are confined to a cube (−0.5, 0.5) in each dimension, supporting frequency-invariant array responses within the operational band.
Increasing the number of symmetric focal points narrows the main lobe and suppresses sidelobes without compromising steering capability or frequency invariance.

paper_title: An adaptive LTE listen-before-talk scheme towards a fair coexistence with Wi-Fi in unlicensed spectrum
paper_claims: Fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access channel occupancy durations that are much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence in co-channel deployments.

An adaptive LTE‑U listen‑before‑talk mechanism that performs clear channel assessment before each burst and then uses a tunable transmission opportunity followed by a tunable muting period, with subframe‑aligned reservation signaling, enables fair, technology‑agnostic coexistence with Wi‑Fi and other unlicensed systems by balancing access opportunities according to network density and traffic requirements while preserving time‑critical LTE signaling on licensed spectrum.

Fair coexistence in unlicensed bands is more appropriately defined as equal sharing of wireless resources among co‑located networks rather than among different radio technologies, with throughput and channel occupancy serving as practical fairness indicators.

paper_title: In vivo estimation of passive biomechanical properties of human myocardium
paper_claims: Maintaining fixed ratios among the anisotropic parameters (af:as:afs and bf:bs:bfs) and estimating only four scalars (a, b, Ka, Kb) preserves the experimentally observed shear stiffness ordering and suffices to calibrate the model from limited data.

Identified passive myocardial parameters depend more strongly on the assumed left-ventricular end-diastolic pressure than on ventricular geometry or fibre helix angle, with the stiffness nonlinearity parameter b being most sensitive.
In vivo human myocardial passive properties are less stiff than pig-derived or ex vivo human measurements, which otherwise yield unrealistically stiff diastolic behavior in human ventricular models.

Across subjects and within physiological variations of pressure and fibre orientation, the predicted shear stress–strain behavior in dominant shear modes remains similar when parameters are constrained to the estimated ranges.

paper_title: Approximation of quantum control correction scheme using deep neural networks
paper_claims: Bidirectional long short-term memory networks approximate the functional mapping from normal control pulses for an ideal two-qubit system to de-noising pulses that compensate coherent drift, enabling generation of effective controls without explicit system modelling.

The learnability and accuracy of the NCP-to-DCP mapping depend on the drift structure, being easier when the drift Hamiltonian resembles the base spin-chain interaction and harder for asymmetric single-qubit drifts orthogonal to the available controls.

The learned mapping exhibits continuity and approximate symmetry with respect to local perturbations of the input pulses, as small changes in control amplitudes at nearby times or along different control axes induce statistically similar variations in the corrected pulses over mid evolution windows.
For weak Markovian incoherent noise modeled by Lindblad operators, learning-based correction of control pulses does not provide a meaningful performance gain over uncorrected pulses.

Optimizing for robustness by training on Gaussian-amplitude–perturbed copies of the generated pulses fails to produce controls with improved fluctuation resilience, indicating limitations of gradient-based learning for this robustness objective.
